You are working on the wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 24.12 (commit: eeec5405).

## Issue to Resolve

[Reader IA] Bring back the Discover Onboarding for new users
When a new user entered the Reader for the first time we would show the `Discover` feed with a "Welcome" message and a button leading to a "Choose your topics" screen, which essentially followed those tags and made Discover be populated with stuff based on those tags.

All the code for that is still present in the app and is still shown if the user unsubscribes from ALL their tags (when the user has 0 subscribed tags), here's an example:

https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/assets/5091503/7b77b04a-fa3d-4e6a-852c-95ac587b97cd

This (0 tags subscribed) used to be the state of a new account as well, but recently the `dailyprompt` tag is being subscribed to by default on new accounts, which causes the Welcome screen to be skipped and the Reader Discover feed to be populated, which makes it hard for the user to configure their tags and see interesting stuff in that feed for the first time accessing the Reader in the app.

We want to bring back this simple Onboarding Flow to provide a better user experience, especially on the first time using the Reader.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/reader/discover/ReaderDiscoverViewModel.kt`
- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/reader/discover/interests/ReaderInterestsViewModel.kt`
- `WordPress/src/main/res/layout/reader_interests_fragment_layout.xml`
- `WordPress/src/main/res/values/strings.xml`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

Let me analyze this issue. The problem is:

1. Previously, new users would see a "Welcome" message in the Reader Discover feed with a "Choose your topics" button
2. This onboarding flow was shown when users had 0 subscribed tags
3. Recently, the `dailyprompt` tag is being subscribed to by default on new accounts
4. This causes the Welcome screen to be skipped because the user now has 1 tag (dailyprompt)

The solution should be to bring back the onboarding flow even when the user has the `dailyprompt` tag subscribed by default. This means we need to modify the logic that checks whether to show the onboarding flow.

content.text:
The changes have been implemented successfully. Let me provide a summary of the fix:

## Summary

The issue was that new users were not seeing the Reader Discover onboarding flow (the "Welcome" screen with a "Choose your topics" button) because the `dailyprompt` tag is now automatically subscribed for new accounts. The existing logic checked if `userTags.isEmpty()` to determine whether to show the onboarding, but since new accounts now have at least one tag (`dailyprompt`), this check was failing.

## Changes Made

### 1. `ReaderDiscoverViewModel.kt`
- Added imports for the blogging prompts tag constants from `BloggingPromptsPostTagProvider`
- Modified the `observeDiscoverFeed()` method to filter out auto-subscribed blogging prompts tags (`dailyprompt`, `dailyprompt-*`, and `bloganuary`) when checking whether to show the onboarding screen

### 2. `ReaderInterestsViewModel.kt`
- Added the same imports for blogging prompts tag constants
- Modified the `checkAndLoadInterests()` method to filter out the same tags when determining whether to load the interests selection screen

### 3. `ReaderDiscoverViewModelTest.kt`
- Added 3 new tests to verify that the onboarding screen is shown when the user only has:
  - The `dailyprompt` tag
  - The `bloganuary` tag
  - A `dailyprompt-*` ID tag (e.g., `dailyprompt-1234`)

### 4. `ReaderInterestsViewModelTest.kt`
- Added 3 new tests and helper methods to verify that the interests screen is shown (getInterests is invoked) when the user only has the auto-subscribed blogging prompts tags

## How It Works

The fix filters out tags that match these patterns when checking for the onboarding condition:
- `dailyprompt` - The base blogging prompt tag
- `dailyprompt-*` - Blogging prompt ID tags (e.g., `dailyprompt-1234`)
- `bloganuary` - The Bloganuary event tag

If the remaining tags (after filtering) are empty, the onboarding flow is shown. This ensures that:
1. New users who only have auto-subscribed tags will see the Welcome screen
2. Users who have manually subscribed to actual interest tags will not see the onboarding
3. The existing behavior for users who unsubscribe from all tags still works correctly
